title: Git's blind spot
purpose: Show that a stack can rebase cleanly while an agent-generated change was planned against architecture assumptions that no longer hold.

title: GPT-5.6 plans a real stack
purpose: Run the refresh-token task and show GPT-5.6 produce U1 schema, U2 runtime, and U3 API work units backed by real commits and GraphBlueprint snapshots.

title: High-level semantic review
purpose: Use the GitLens-style graph to select commits and review intent, architectural delta, affected capabilities, dependencies, checks, and optional raw diffs.

title: Architecture changes without a text conflict
purpose: Advance main so session writes require a broker, perform a clean Git rebase, and focus the selected commit on the blueprint and claim-impact delta.

title: Deterministic selective invalidation
purpose: Show U1 remain valid while GateResult invalidates U2 and dependent U3 with exact witnesses, evidence, checks, and reason codes.

title: Selective replan and recovery
purpose: Ask GPT-5.6 to replan only U2 and U3, bind fresh witnesses, rerun deterministic checks, and restore a green gate without model self-grading.

title: Evidence and impact
purpose: Export the redacted bundle, distinguish claim from evidence and gate, explain Codex/GPT-5.6/GraphReFly roles, and close on review compression rather than blind trust.
